commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=27f4dc80fe47c439d37b213c68acc8ff3f4293eb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Mattermost thread-context construction and direct send-action fallback logic so `message(action="send")` can inherit an active Mattermost thread, with regression tests around reply precedence and replyToMode behavior.

PR surface: Source +52, Tests +186. Total +238 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: combine the PR's `buildToolContext` output with current `handleSendAction`, which writes `params.replyTo` from `currentMessageId` before dispatching to Mattermost. I did not run a live Mattermost setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor or maintainer repair plus real Mattermost proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's live-environment proof gate.

target.title: fix(mattermost): inherit message tool thread context
target.body:
## Summary
- port the stale PR #52120 Mattermost thread-context behavior onto current `main`
- add Mattermost `threading.buildToolContext` so active thread metadata reaches the shared message tool path
- make direct `message(action=send)` Mattermost sends inherit the active same-channel thread when `replyToMode` allows it, while keeping explicit `replyTo` ahead of implicit context

Supersedes #52120.

## Verification
- `node --check extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/test-helpers/surface-contract-suite.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm check:changed`
- `CI=true pnpm check:architecture`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost `message(action=send)` calls from an active Mattermost thread lost the inbound thread context unless the caller supplied an explicit `replyTo`/`replyToId`, so assistant replies could escape to the channel root.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw checkout on Raspberry Pi arm64, Node v22.22.2, pnpm v11.2.2, current `main` plus this patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node --check extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/test-helpers/surface-contract-suite.ts
CI=true pnpm check:changed
CI=true pnpm check:architecture
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the patched checkout showed the Mattermost action path sends the inherited thread id and the changed-file/architecture guards finish cleanly:
```text
extension-mattermost extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts (46 checks)
  inherits the active Mattermost thread for message tool sends without explicit replyTo -> replyToId "thread-root-id"
  keeps explicit replyTo ahead of the active Mattermost thread -> replyToId "explicit-root"
  does not inherit the active Mattermost thread across channels -> replyToId undefined
  consumes replyToMode=first after the first Mattermost message tool threaded send -> hasRepliedRef true

[check:changed] lanes=extensions, extensionChecks
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
[check:architecture]
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
Madge import cycle check: 0 cycle(s).
deprecated API usage guard passed
deprecated JSDoc guard passed
Kysely guardrails OK
```

Observed result after fix: Mattermost sends in the active same-channel thread now pass `replyToId: "thread-root-id"`; explicit `replyTo` still wins, different channels stay unthreaded, and `replyToMode: "first"` flips `hasRepliedRef` after the first threaded send.

What was not tested: A live external Mattermost server post was not sent from this branch; the local runtime path and plugin contract path were exercised in the OpenClaw checkout.
